doc.body.details: is a micro PHP framework with a basic layer separation structure, designed for small projects and landing pages.
doc.features.helpers: Helpers for session, response, database, language, etc.
doc.features.simple: Perfect for simple hosting environments without the need for large frameworks
doc.description.purpose: This project was designed for developers who need a lightweight and practical structure to organize their files and reuse resources across pages and small systems.
doc.database.code.comment.is: compares the active driver with the given value
doc.database.code.comment.ismysql: runs MySQL-specific logic only when configured
doc.database.code.comment.ispostgres: runs PostgreSQL-specific logic only when configured
doc.database.code.comment.isnone: detects when database access is disabled
doc.database.code.comment.defaultport: returns the default port for a driver
doc.database.code.comment.connectionscon: resolves all configured database connection configs
doc.database.code.comment.hasconnection: checks whether a database connection is configured
doc.database.env.desc: Returns the normalized database driver from `DB_DRIVER`: `mysql`, `pgsql`, or `none` when the value is missing or invalid.
doc.database.is.desc: Compares the active database driver with the provided value and returns a boolean. Use it for driver-specific branches before opening connections or running SQL.
doc.database.ismysql.desc: Returns true when the active database driver is `mysql`. Use it for MySQL-only SQL, schema setup, or connection behavior.
doc.database.ispostgres.desc: Returns true when the active database driver is `pgsql`. Use it for PostgreSQL-only SQL, schema setup, or connection behavior.
doc.database.isnone.desc: Returns true when no supported database driver is configured. Check this before code that requires database access.
doc.database.permitteddrivers.desc: Returns the supported PDO driver identifiers currently accepted by the framework: `mysql` and `pgsql`.
doc.database.normalizeconnectionname.des: Trims and lowercases a connection name, returning `default` when the input is empty. Use it when building connection-aware internals.
doc.database.defaultport.desc: Returns the fallback port for supported drivers: `3306` for MySQL, `5432` for PostgreSQL, or null for unknown values.
doc.database.connectionconfig.desc: Returns one normalized connection config from env or runtime registration, or null when that connection is not configured.
doc.database.connectionsconfig.desc: Returns all normalized connection configs available from the default env group, suffixed env groups, and runtime registrations.
doc.database.connectionnames.desc: Returns configured connection names, including `default` when configured, suffixed env connections such as `app` or `auth`, and runtime-registered connections.
doc.database.hasconnection.desc: Returns true when the given connection name resolves to a complete database configuration.
doc.session.env.desc: Returns the normalized session driver from `SESSION_DRIVER`: `files`, `db`, or `none` when the value is missing or invalid.
doc.session.code.comment.is: compares the active session driver with the given value
doc.session.is.desc: Compares the configured session driver with the provided value and returns a boolean. Use it before driver-specific session setup.
doc.session.isfiles.desc: Returns true when sessions are configured to use PHP's file storage under the configured session path.
doc.session.isdb.desc: Returns true when sessions are configured to use the database session handler.
doc.session.isnone.desc: Returns true when sessions are disabled or invalid. Web session reads and writes will throw when this is active.
doc.environment.env.desc: Returns the normalized runtime environment: `production`, `development`, or `testing`. Unknown values fall back to `production`.
doc.environment.code.comment.is: compares the active environment with the given value
doc.environment.is.desc: Compares the active environment with the provided value and returns a boolean. Use it for environment-specific behavior.
doc.environment.code.comment.isproductio: checks whether production behavior should be used
doc.environment.isproduction.desc: Returns true when the environment resolves to `production`, including the fallback for unknown values.
doc.environment.code.comment.isdevelopme: checks whether development behavior should be used
doc.environment.isdevelopment.desc: Returns true when the environment resolves to `development`, usually for detailed errors and local diagnostics.
doc.environment.code.comment.istesting: checks whether testing behavior should be used
doc.environment.istesting.desc: Returns true when the environment resolves to `testing`, useful for test-only configuration and assertions.
doc.globals.code.comment.get: reads a runtime config value or the full config array
doc.globals.code.comment.add: adds or replaces one runtime config value
doc.globals.code.comment.loadenv: loads .env into the internal environment cache
doc.globals.code.comment.env: reads one .env value or the full environment cache
doc.globals.code.comment.isapirequest: checks the current URI against the app API prefix
doc.globals.code.comment.issystemwebrequ: checks the current URI against the system web prefix
doc.globals.code.comment.issystemapirequ: checks the current URI against the system API prefix
doc.globals.get.desc: Returns a runtime config value, the provided default when missing, or the full config array when no key is passed.
doc.globals.add.desc: Adds or overwrites one value in runtime config. Use it for request-level settings that should be available globally.
doc.globals.merge.desc: Merges an associative array into runtime config. Existing keys are overwritten by the new values.
doc.globals.forget.desc: Removes one key from runtime config without touching loaded `.env` values.
doc.globals.forgetmany.desc: Removes several runtime config keys in one call. Pass a simple list of key names.
doc.globals.reset.desc: Clears runtime config and reloads `.env`. Use mainly in bootstrap or tests because it changes global state.
doc.globals.loadenv.desc: Loads `.env` values through Dotenv and caches them internally. Repeated calls are safe once the cache is populated.
doc.globals.env.desc: Returns one loaded environment value, `null` when missing, or the full env array when no key is passed.
doc.globals.getapiprefix.desc: Returns the app API prefix, currently `/api`, for app route loading and request detection.
doc.globals.isapirequest.desc: Returns true when the current request URI targets the app API prefix after removing `BASE_PATH`.
doc.globals.getsystemwebprefix.desc: Returns the system web prefix, currently `/web-system`, where framework-owned web routes such as the documentation home are loaded.
doc.globals.getsystemapiprefix.desc: Returns the system API prefix, currently `/api-system`, for framework-owned API routes.
doc.globals.issystemwebrequest.desc: Returns true when the current request URI targets the system web prefix after removing `BASE_PATH`.
doc.globals.issystemapirequest.desc: Returns true when the current request URI targets the system API prefix after removing `BASE_PATH`. System API requests are handled without sessions.
doc.autoload.code.comment.from: requires every PHP file from a valid directory
doc.autoload.from.desc: Recursively scans a directory and `require_once`s each `.php` file. Use it during bootstrap for helper or bootstrapping folders; it throws if the directory is invalid.
doc.autoload.boot.desc: Scans `app/Bootable` and calls `boot()` on classes that implement `System\Interfaces\IBootable`.
doc.router_loader.load.desc: Loads a route file from `app/routes` without a prefix. Use it for application-owned route files.
doc.router_loader.code.comment.loadWithP: loads an app route file under a prefix
doc.router_loader.loadWithPrefix.desc: Loads a route file from `app/routes` inside a route group with the provided URL prefix.
doc.router_loader.loadSystem.desc: Loads a route file from `system/routes` without a prefix. Use it only for framework-owned route files.
doc.router_loader.code.comment.loadSyste: loads a system route file under a prefix
doc.router_loader.loadSystemWithPrefix.d: Loads a route file from `system/routes` inside a route group with the provided URL prefix.
doc.router_loader.dispatch.desc: Dispatches the current HTTP method and URI through the loaded route collector and emits the matched PSR-7 response.
doc.database.code.comment.connect: opens or returns the cached PDO connection
doc.database.code.comment.configconfigur: registers a runtime config without touching PDO cache
doc.database.code.comment.configforgetco: removes a runtime config without touching PDO cache
doc.database.code.comment.statement: executes prepared write or DDL SQL
doc.database.code.comment.select: fetches all rows from a prepared SELECT
doc.database.code.comment.selectrow: fetches the first row or selected column
doc.database.code.comment.getlastinserte: reads the last insert ID from the active PDO connection
doc.database.code.comment.isintransactio: checks the framework transaction nesting level
doc.database.code.comment.starttransacti: starts a transaction or creates a savepoint
doc.database.code.comment.committransact: commits or releases the latest savepoint
doc.database.code.comment.rollbacktransa: rolls back or returns to the latest savepoint
doc.database.connect.desc: Opens and returns the cached PDO connection for `default` or a named connection such as `app`, `auth`, or `robot`. Named env connections use suffixed `DB_*_<NAME>` variables and connect lazily.
doc.database.configconfigure.desc: Registers or replaces only the configuration-level runtime connection. Prefer `database_configure()` for application code because it also resets any cached PDO connection.
doc.database.configforgetconnection.desc: Removes only the configuration-level runtime connection. Prefer `database_forget_connection()` for application code because it also closes any cached PDO connection.
doc.database.configure.desc: Registers or replaces a runtime connection that is not defined in env. Required fields are `driver`, `host`, `name`, and `user`; `pass`, `port`, and `charset` are optional.
doc.database.forgetconnection.desc: Removes a runtime connection config and closes any cached PDO instance for that connection.
doc.database.statement.desc: Executes prepared SQL for inserts, updates, deletes, DDL, or other statements on the selected connection and returns true on success. Always pass user data through parameters.
doc.database.select.desc: Runs a prepared SELECT on the selected connection and returns an array of rows. When `$key` is provided, it returns that column from each row instead of full rows.
doc.database.selectrow.desc: Runs a prepared SELECT on the selected connection and returns the first row, one column value when `$key` is provided, or `null` when no row is found.
doc.database.disconnect.desc: Closes one cached PDO connection when a name is provided, or all cached connections when omitted. Use it in tests, long-running scripts, or teardown code.
doc.database.getlastinsertedid.desc: Returns PDO's last generated insert ID from the selected connection as a string, or false when the driver cannot provide one. Call it immediately after an insert on the same connection.
doc.database.isintransaction.desc: Returns true when the selected connection has an active transaction or nested savepoint. Useful before rollback in catch blocks.
doc.database.starttransaction.desc: Starts the selected connection's outer PDO transaction, or creates a savepoint when already inside one. Use it to group related writes safely.
doc.database.committransaction.desc: Commits the selected connection's outer transaction, or releases the latest savepoint for nested calls. Returns true or throws on failure.
doc.database.rollbacktransaction.desc: Rolls back the selected connection's outer transaction, or rolls back to and releases the latest savepoint for nested calls. Returns true or throws on failure.
doc.language.code.comment.get.all: returns loaded translations or one translated key
doc.language.code.comment.get.key: returns a specific translated string by key
doc.language.code.comment.getByPrefix: returns translations filtered by a key prefix
doc.language.code.comment.load: loads a language or detects one automatically
doc.language.code.comment.detect: detects language from HTTP headers and fallback config
doc.language.get.desc: Returns one translated string, all loaded translations when no key is passed, or `null` when the key is missing. Replacements such as `{name}` are applied when provided.
doc.language.getByPrefix.desc: Loads the requested language with the normal fallback rules and returns only translations whose full keys start with the normalized prefix.
doc.language.current.desc: Returns the language code currently loaded by the framework, such as `en` or `pt-br`, or `null` before loading.
doc.language.load.desc: Loads translation JSON files for the requested language from `app/languages` and `system/languages`. When no language is passed, it detects the browser language and falls back to the configured default.
doc.language.default.desc: Returns the configured default language from `DEFAULT_LANGUAGE` in lowercase, or `null` when it is not set.
doc.language.detect.desc: Reads `HTTP_ACCEPT_LANGUAGE`, tries the full and short language codes, and falls back to `DEFAULT_LANGUAGE`.
doc.language.normalizePrefix.desc: Trims leading and trailing dots and returns the prefix with exactly one trailing dot, or an empty string when the input is empty.
doc.path.code.comment.root: returns the project root filesystem path
doc.path.root.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to the project root. Use it when building paths outside `app`, `system`, or `public`.
doc.path.app.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app`, where application controllers, models, routes, helpers, and views live.
doc.path.code.comment.appHelpers: returns the app helper directory path
doc.path.appHelpers.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/helpers`, useful when loading or checking application helper files.
doc.path.code.comment.appLanguages: returns the app language directory path
doc.path.appLanguages.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/languages`, where application-owned translation JSON files live.
doc.path.code.comment.appRoutes: returns the app route directory path
doc.path.appRoutes.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/routes`, where web and API route files are loaded from.
doc.path.code.comment.appControllers: returns the app controller directory path
doc.path.appControllers.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/Controllers`, where namespaced application controllers are stored.
doc.path.code.comment.appModels: returns the app model directory path
doc.path.appModels.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/Models`, where application data access classes are stored.
doc.path.code.comment.appViews: returns the app view directory path
doc.path.appViews.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/views`, the root for page views and templates.
doc.path.code.comment.appViewsPages: returns the page view directory path
doc.path.appViewsPages.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/views/pages`, where `view_render_page()` resolves page files.
doc.path.code.comment.appViewsTemplates: returns the template view directory path
doc.path.appViewsTemplates.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/views/templates`, where layout templates used by `View` are stored.
doc.path.code.comment.system: returns the framework system directory path
doc.path.system.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system`, where framework core classes, config classes, helpers, includes, and interfaces live.
doc.path.code.comment.systemHelpers: returns the system helper directory path
doc.path.systemHelpers.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/helpers`, the internal helper folder loaded during bootstrap.
doc.path.code.comment.systemLanguages: returns the system language directory path
doc.path.systemLanguages.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/languages`, where framework, documentation, template, validation, and core error translations live.
doc.path.code.comment.systemRoutes: returns the system route directory path
doc.path.systemRoutes.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/routes`, where framework-owned web and API route files live.
doc.path.code.comment.systemMiddlewares: returns the system middleware directory path
doc.path.systemMiddlewares.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/Middlewares`, where framework-owned route middleware classes live.
doc.path.code.comment.systemControllers: returns the system controller directory path
doc.path.systemControllers.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/Controllers`, where framework-owned controllers such as `Home` and `I18n` live.
doc.path.code.comment.systemModels: returns the system model directory path
doc.path.systemModels.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/Models`, reserved for framework-owned model and data classes.
doc.path.code.comment.systemViews: returns the system view directory path
doc.path.systemViews.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/views`, the root for framework-owned pages and templates.
doc.path.code.comment.systemViewsPages: returns the system page view directory path
doc.path.systemViewsPages.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/views/pages`, where `View::render_system_page()` resolves system page files.
doc.path.code.comment.systemViewsTemplat: returns the system template view directory path
doc.path.systemViewsTemplates.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/views/templates`, where system layout templates are stored.
doc.path.code.comment.systemIncludes: returns the system include directory path
doc.path.systemIncludes.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/includes`, used for internal bootstrap includes such as error and session handlers.
doc.path.public.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `public`, the expected web document root containing `index.php` and public assets.
doc.path.storage.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `storage`, used for framework storage such as logs, sessions, and AI context files.
doc.path.storageSessions.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `storage/sessions`, where file-backed sessions are stored when enabled.
doc.path.storageLogs.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `storage/logs`, where daily exception logs are written outside production.
doc.path.code.comment.languages: returns the legacy root language directory path
doc.path.languages.desc: Returns the legacy root `languages` path. Runtime translation loading now uses `app/languages` and `system/languages`.
doc.path.basePath.desc: Returns the normalized URL base path from `BASE_PATH`, with a leading slash and no trailing slash, or an empty string for root installs.
doc.path.basePathPublic.desc: Returns the normalized public asset base path by appending `/public` to the configured base path. Use it for asset URLs in views.
doc.path.code.comment.siteURL: builds an absolute URL with optional suffix
doc.path.siteURL.desc: Builds an absolute URL from the current host, protocol, `BASE_PATH`, and optional relative suffix. Use it for redirects and links.
doc.response.redirect.desc: Returns a PSR-7 redirect response. Relative URLs are converted with `siteURL()`, and the status code is selected automatically unless provided.
doc.response.html.desc: Returns a PSR-7 HTML response with the provided body and status code. Use it for rendered pages from web routes.
doc.response.code.comment.text: returns a PSR-7 plain text response
doc.response.text.desc: Returns a PSR-7 plain text response with the provided body and status code. Use it for simple messages or fallback errors.
doc.response.json.desc: Returns a PSR-7 JSON response. Arrays are encoded automatically; strings are treated as already encoded JSON.
doc.response.xml.desc: Returns a PSR-7 response with `application/xml; charset=utf-8` and the selected status code.
doc.response.code.comment.file: returns a PSR-7 file download response
doc.response.file.desc: Returns a PSR-7 download response for a readable file, including content length, content disposition, ETag, and hash headers. Missing files return a text error response.
doc.session.code.comment.start: starts the web session when allowed
doc.session.start.desc: Starts the PHP session when a web session driver is enabled. It throws when sessions are disabled or the current request is an API request.
doc.session.code.comment.has: checks whether a session key exists
doc.session.has.desc: Starts the session if needed and returns true when the key exists in `$_SESSION`.
doc.session.code.comment.get: reads a session value with fallback
doc.session.get.desc: Starts the session if needed and returns the stored value, or the provided default when the key does not exist.
doc.session.set.desc: Starts the session if needed and stores one key/value pair in `$_SESSION`.
doc.session.setMany.desc: Starts the session if needed and stores each key/value pair from the provided associative array.
doc.session.forget.desc: Starts the session if needed and removes the given key from `$_SESSION`.
doc.session.clear.desc: Starts the session if needed and clears all session values while keeping the session itself alive.
doc.session.code.comment.save: writes and closes the active session
doc.session.save.desc: Writes and closes the active session when one is open. Use it to release the session lock before long work.
doc.session.code.comment.destroy: clears and destroys the active session
doc.session.destroy.desc: Destroys the active session when one is open, clears `$_SESSION`, and removes server-side session state.
doc.session.regenerate.desc: Starts the session if needed and regenerates its ID. Use it after login or privilege changes to reduce fixation risk.
doc.view.code.comment.share: shares one variable with future renders
doc.view.share.desc: Stores one shared variable that is extracted into every rendered page or template until it is forgotten or cleared.
doc.view.code.comment.shareMany: shares several variables with future renders
doc.view.shareMany.desc: Stores multiple shared variables from an associative array. Local render data can still provide page-specific values.
doc.view.forget.desc: Removes one shared variable from the global view scope so later renders no longer receive it.
doc.view.forgetMany.desc: Removes each variable named in the provided list from the global view scope.
doc.view.clear.desc: Clears every shared view variable. Use it in tests or isolated render flows that must not inherit previous state.
doc.view.code.comment.setTemplate: selects the layout template for renders
doc.view.setTemplate.desc: Sets the template path relative to `app/views/templates`, with or without `.php`. Empty values fall back to `/template.php`.
doc.view.code.comment.getTemplate: returns the current normalized template path
doc.view.getTemplate.desc: Returns the current template path, initializing it to `/template.php` when no template has been selected.
doc.view.code.comment.renderPage: renders a page view inside the template
doc.view.renderPage.desc: Renders a page from `app/views/pages` inside the current template and returns the final HTML string. Pass `$data` for local variables.
doc.view.code.comment.renderSystemPage: renders a system page view inside the system template
doc.view.renderSystemPage.desc: Renders a page from `system/views/pages` inside the system template and returns the final HTML string. Pass `$data` for local variables.
doc.view.code.comment.renderHtml: renders raw HTML inside the template
doc.view.renderHtml.desc: Renders a raw HTML string inside the current template and returns the final HTML string. Useful for generated content that still needs the layout.
doc.view.code.comment.renderVue: renders an optional Vue page inside the PHP template
doc.view.renderVue.desc: Renders an optional Vue page from `resources/vue/pages` inside the current PHP template. The page argument is relative to that folder, data becomes Vue props, the entrypoint is relative to `resources/vue`, and `null` uses `main.js`. Pass one i18n prefix or a prefix list to let the Vue entrypoint fetch translations from `/api-system/i18n` with `X-System-Token`. Asset URLs and i18n endpoints preserve `BASE_PATH`.
doc.view.getGlobals.desc: Returns the associative array of variables shared through `share()` and `shareMany()`.
doc.form_validator.validate.desc: Validates the resolved form data against pipe-separated rules and returns true only when every field passes. Errors are stored by field path.
doc.form_validator.register_rule.desc: Registers a custom validation rule callable. The callback can return true, false, null, or a custom error message string.
doc.form_validator.setForm.desc: Sets an explicit data array for validation instead of falling back to `$_POST` and then `$_GET`.
doc.form_validator.resetErrors.desc: Clears the current error list without changing the form data or registered custom rules.
doc.form_validator.errors.desc: Returns the validation errors collected by the latest `validate()` call, keyed by resolved field path.
doc.form_validator.code.comment.get: reads a top-level field with default fallback
doc.form_validator.get.desc: Returns a top-level field value from the resolved form source, or the provided default when it is missing.
doc.form_validator.code.comment.has: checks whether a top-level field exists
doc.form_validator.has.desc: Returns true when the top-level field exists in the resolved form source.
doc.path.code.comment.appBootable: returns the bootable class directory path
doc.path.appBootable.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/Bootable`, where classes with automatic startup logic are stored.
doc.path.code.comment.appMiddlewares: returns the app middleware directory path
doc.path.appMiddlewares.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `app/Middlewares`, where route middleware classes are stored.
doc.path.code.comment.systemInterfaces: returns the system interface directory path
doc.path.systemInterfaces.desc: Returns the absolute filesystem path to `system/Interfaces`, where framework contracts such as `IBootable` are stored.
doc.helper_loading.title: Helper loading
doc.helper_loading.system: `SYSTEM_HELPERS_AUTOLOAD` controls optional wrappers from `system/helpers` independently from app helpers.
doc.helper_loading.app: `APP_HELPERS_AUTOLOAD` controls helpers from `app/helpers`; application code may still use helpers when they are enabled.
doc.helper_loading.runtime: Framework runtime code uses static system classes directly, so it does not require system helpers to be loaded.
doc.cleanup.title: Dangerous app cleanup
doc.cleanup.description: Removes example app files, clears selected asset and runtime folders, resets app routes, and copies the system template into the app template folder.
doc.cleanup.modal.title: Remove and clean the app MVC?
doc.cleanup.modal.warning: This action is irreversible and deletes the contents of these paths.
doc.cleanup.modal.routes: The app web route will be reset to redirect to /web-system, and the app API route will be cleared.
doc.cleanup.success.title: MVC cleaned
doc.cleanup.success.text: The app skeleton was cleaned and the template was copied.
doc.cleanup.error.title: Cleanup failed
doc.cleanup.error.text: The cleanup request could not be completed.
